feature: SEC-3 synthetic/test bucket and evidence boundaries
summary: Workstream 3: Runtime Validation and Fail-Fast Enforcement. Classification validation module provides bucket/prefix naming checks, classification derivation, and fail-fast guards (mixed-classification export rejection, storage target mismatch rejection). Pre-existing SEC3-F-P3-02a (synthetic output namespace guard) already covers materialization request/completion flows. All new validation functions have dedicated test modules that pass.
assertions: classify_bucket_name identifies synthetic_test bucket prefixes (dq-test-data, dq-landing-zone, dq-preview, dq-demo)
classify_bucket_name identifies real_evidence bucket prefixes (dq-evidence, dq-reporting, dq-source, dq-delivery)
classify_bucket_name returns unclassified for non-conforming bucket names
classify_uri parses s3a:// and s3:// URIs into bucket and key prefix
classify_uri returns unclassified for unparseable URIs
validate_bucket_naming accepts valid synthetic/test bucket with compliant key prefix
validate_bucket_naming accepts valid real/evidence bucket with compliant key prefix
validate_bucket_naming rejects synthetic/test bucket with prohibited key prefix terms (evidence, reporting, compliance)
validate_bucket_naming rejects real/evidence bucket with prohibited key prefix terms (test, synthetic, preview)
validate_bucket_naming rejects bucket when expected_classification does not match actual classification
ensure_no_mixed_classification allows empty delivery notes list
ensure_no_mixed_classification allows uniform classification across notes
ensure_no_mixed_classification rejects mixed synthetic/test and real/evidence classifications
ensure_no_mixed_classification rejects synthetic artifacts when consumer_scope is real_evidence
ensure_storage_target_matches rejects mismatched classification with context details
pre-existing SEC3-F-P3-02a ensures synthetic output namespace guard rejects evidence-style URIs
